_meta.source: Tier-1 allowlist pool, web-scored by ECC researchers; point-in-time snapshot
_meta.note: popularity/maintenance are as-of the snapshot date; regenerate via the discovery+scoring loop.

capabilities.what_it_is: AWS-official MCP server for searching and reading AWS documentation pages (4 tools)
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by AWS Labs; Docker Scout health scoring applied; no known CVEs noted on catalog page
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 100K+ Docker pulls, AWS-official, essential for any AWS-heavy workflow; broad utility across cloud users

capabilities.what_it_is: Dynatrace-official MCP server providing 16 tools for querying DQL, metrics, logs, spans, and Dynatrace workflows
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by Dynatrace OSS org; requires Dynatrace API token — token should be scoped minimally; MIT license
capabilities.deluxe_reason: High-value for Dynatrace shops but niche; Grafana/Datadog cover overlapping observability use-cases more broadly

capabilities.what_it_is: Grafana-official MCP server with 56 tools for dashboards, datasources, Prometheus metrics, and Grafana API access
capabilities.security_note: Apache-2.0 license; COSIGN signature verification available; Docker Scout health scoring applied; 3K+ GitHub stars
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 500K+ Docker pulls, Grafana-official, 56 tools, broadly useful for observability and monitoring workflows

capabilities.what_it_is: Heroku-official MCP server with 34 tools for app deployment, dyno scaling, add-ons, pipelines, and PostgreSQL management
capabilities.security_note: Apache-2.0 license; requires HEROKU_API_KEY env var; delegates to Heroku CLI internally — ensure CLI version >=10.8.1
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful for Heroku users but niche; 8.4K pulls places it below threshold for a curated starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: MCP server for Kubernetes cluster management with 23 tools (deploy, scale, exec, Helm); maintained by Flux159
capabilities.security_note: MIT license; requires kubeconfig access — high-privilege tool, scope RBAC carefully; Docker Scout health scoring applied
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 50K+ Docker pulls, widely used, covers a critical infrastructure surface for cloud-native teams

capabilities.what_it_is: Okta-official MCP server for IAM operations — user/group management and Okta admin API access via AI agents
capabilities.security_note: Apache-2.0; official Okta repo; security-sensitive — requires Okta API token with admin scope; restrict to read-only where possible
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Security-sensitive IAM tooling with low pull count; too niche and high-risk for a generic starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: Stripe-official MCP server with 22 tools for interacting with Stripe API (payments, customers, subscriptions, etc.)
capabilities.security_note: MIT license; requires STRIPE_SECRET_KEY — never use live keys in dev; Stripe also hosts a remote OAuth MCP at mcp.stripe.com
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Stripe-official, 10K+ pulls, payments integration is a common need in product development workflows

capabilities.what_it_is: AWS Labs monorepo of specialized MCP servers for AWS services (documentation, IaC, CloudWatch, and more)
capabilities.security_note: Official AWS source; servers are individual packages — review IAM permissions per-server; SSE support removed in latest major versions (stdio-only)
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official AWS umbrella server collection, actively maintained as of April 2026, critical for AWS-centric agent workflows

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Microsoft Azure MCP server (from Azure/azure-mcp, now microsoft/mcp) for managing Azure resources via AI agents
capabilities.security_note: Official Microsoft image at mcr.microsoft.com; original Azure/azure-mcp repo archived Aug 2025, active development moved to microsoft/mcp; supports .mcpb bundle install
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Microsoft/Azure source, full Microsoft Learn documentation, essential for Azure-centric infrastructure workflows

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Cloudflare MCP server for managing Cloudflare account resources (DNS, Workers, security) via natural language
capabilities.security_note: Apache-2.0; cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare repo; also offers remote MCP at mcp.cloudflare.com; requires Cloudflare API token
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Cloudflare vendor server, broadly adopted, covers a major edge/CDN/security platform used by many teams

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Vercel MCP server (remote OAuth) for managing Vercel projects, deployments, and docs search
capabilities.security_note: Remote MCP with OAuth; Vercel controls client allowlist — only approved MCP clients can connect; full docs at vercel.com/docs/mcp/vercel-mcp
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Vercel server, OAuth-secured remote MCP, highly relevant for frontend/fullstack teams deploying on Vercel

capabilities.what_it_is: Community MCP server (sooperset) for Atlassian Confluence and Jira with 73 tools; supports Cloud and Server/Data Center
capabilities.security_note: MIT license; community-maintained (not official Atlassian); 100K+ Docker pulls; requires Atlassian API token — scope to minimum needed permissions
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 100K+ pulls, 73 tools, very broad adoption — de-facto standard for Atlassian MCP integration despite being community-built

capabilities.what_it_is: Dart AI project management MCP server by its-dart; local version deprecated in favor of hosted Dart MCP server
capabilities.security_note: Local server version explicitly deprecated by maintainer; users redirected to hosted version — review hosted server's data handling before use
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Local version deprecated; low popularity (37 GitHub stars); Dart AI is a niche tool — not suitable for curated starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: LINE-official MCP server integrating the LINE Messaging API for push messages, broadcasts, and rich menus via AI agents
capabilities.security_note: Official LINE repo (line/line-bot-mcp-server); requires Channel Access Token — store securely, never in source; 10K+ Docker pulls
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official LINE source with 10K+ pulls, but regionally focused (Japan/Asia) — too niche for a global curated starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: Archived Anthropic reference Slack MCP server (modelcontextprotocol/servers); superseded by Slack's official server
capabilities.security_note: Archived ~May 2025; Anthropic no longer maintains. Use Slack's own official MCP server for new deployments.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Archived and unmaintained; Slack now publishes its own official server that supersedes this.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Teamwork.com MCP server providing 168 tools for Desk, Projects, and Spaces products
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by Teamwork org on GitHub (github.com/Teamwork/mcp); packaged by Docker Inc. No known issues.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Niche project-management SaaS tool; high tool count (168) but limited general audience compared to broader productivity tools.

capabilities.what_it_is: Anthropic-built first-party Gmail connector for Claude; one-click no-setup integration launched July 2025
capabilities.security_note: Official Anthropic connector; vetted by Anthropic for security and compatibility. OAuth-gated.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Anthropic connector for one of the world's most-used email services; zero-config, high everyday utility.

capabilities.what_it_is: Anthropic-built first-party Google Calendar connector for Claude; one-click no-setup integration
capabilities.security_note: Official Anthropic connector; vetted by Anthropic for security and compatibility. OAuth-gated.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Anthropic connector for Google Calendar; high everyday utility for scheduling and time management use cases.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official HubSpot MCP server (remote + local dev variant) for CRM data access via OAuth 2.0; launched public beta May 2025
capabilities.security_note: Official HubSpot product; OAuth 2.0 with user-level scope control. Admins can limit agent data access.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful for HubSpot CRM users but narrow audience; not general-purpose enough for a curated starter set.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official PagerDuty MCP server for incident management, on-call schedules, services, and event orchestrations
capabilities.security_note: Official PagerDuty repo (github.com/PagerDuty/pagerduty-mcp-server); 71 stars, 307 commits, actively developed but no formal releases published yet.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Specialist ops/SRE tool; strong for incident response workflows but limited to PagerDuty users.

capabilities.what_it_is: Community Airtable MCP server by domdomegg (not official Airtable); 16 tools for schema reads, record queries, and attachments
capabilities.security_note: Community-built (not from Airtable Inc.); packaged by Docker Inc. through MCP catalog. MIT License. No known CVEs identified.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful for Airtable users but community-maintained (not official) and has limited audience.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official chroma-core MCP server for ChromaDB vector database; supports semantic search and persistent/cloud storage modes
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by the official Chroma organization (github.com/chroma-core/chroma-mcp); packaged by Docker Inc. No known issues.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official vector DB MCP from chroma-core; enables semantic memory/RAG patterns — high value for AI orchestration stacks.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official ClickHouse MCP server (github.com/ClickHouse/mcp-clickhouse); provides database/table listing and query tools
capabilities.security_note: Apache 2.0 license; maintained by ClickHouse org. Docker image has 10K+ pulls. Packaged by Docker Inc.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Solid official server for ClickHouse users but narrower use case (OLAP analytics DB) than general-purpose data tools.

capabilities.what_it_is: Community CockroachDB MCP server by amineelkouhen; 29 tools for NL query, schema analysis, and cluster management
capabilities.security_note: Community-built (individual maintainer, not CockroachDB Inc.); MIT License; packaged by Docker Inc. Review carefully before production use.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Community-maintained by individual contributor, not official CockroachDB Inc.; limited audience and unverified long-term maintenance commitment.

capabilities.what_it_is: Couchbase-Ecosystem community MCP server for Couchbase clusters; 20 tools, read-only mode by default, SQL++ query support
capabilities.security_note: Ecosystem/community org, not Couchbase Inc. support. Read-only by default is a good safety default. Packaged by Docker Inc.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Ecosystem project with limited audience; not officially supported by Couchbase Inc. engineering team.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Keboola MCP server; open-source bridge to Keboola platform (storage, SQL transforms, job triggers); 33 tools
capabilities.security_note: Official Keboola org (github.com/keboola/mcp-server); Docker image updated within 4 days of research date (June 2026). Token-auth required.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and actively maintained but targets Keboola platform users specifically; too niche for a general starter set.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official MongoDB MCP server by mongodb-js; 24 tools for querying, aggregation, indexing, and Atlas cluster management
capabilities.security_note: Official MongoDB project (github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-mcp-server); Apache 2.0 license; packaged by Docker Inc. Multiple tagged releases through late 2025.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official MongoDB server with high popularity, 24 tools, and broad relevance to developers using the world's most popular document database.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Neo4j MCP server (github.com/neo4j/mcp); Cypher read/write, schema introspection, and GDS procedure listing
capabilities.security_note: Official Neo4j org (github.com/neo4j/mcp); v1.5.3 released June 11 2026; 264 stars. Write mode explicitly flagged as non-production only.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and actively maintained with recent releases, but graph DB use is more specialized than general-purpose relational/document stores.

capabilities.what_it_is: Neon serverless Postgres MCP server: manage projects, branches, run SQL, run migrations via natural language
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official vendor server for a popular serverless Postgres platform with 610 GitHub stars and 10K+ Docker pulls; covers branching-based migrations that fit AI-dev workflows well.
capabilities.security_note: Requires NEON_API_KEY; grants broad database management powers — LLM-initiated actions should be reviewed before execution.

capabilities.what_it_is: SQLite MCP server with 25 tools: relational ops, full-text search, vector search, geospatial, CSV/JSON import
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Community-authored (neverinfamous org), limited verifiable activity; the upstream maintainer deprecated an earlier version in favour of db-mcp, raising continuity concerns.
capabilities.security_note: No credential requirements for local SQLite files, but geospatial/vector extensions increase attack surface if database path is user-controlled.

capabilities.what_it_is: Valkey (Redis-compatible) MCP server: Strings, Lists, Hashes, Streams, JSON ops via AWS Labs implementation
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Valkey is a niche Redis fork; AWS Labs maintains it but adoption is low and most users would pick a Redis MCP instead. Include only if Valkey is explicitly in the stack.
capabilities.security_note: Needs VALKEY_HOST/VALKEY_PORT env vars; ensure Valkey instance is not publicly exposed without auth.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Datadog MCP server: query logs, metrics, traces, incidents via natural language against Datadog APIs
capabilities.deluxe_reason: First-party Datadog server enabling AI-driven observability queries; critical for teams already on Datadog. 41 GitHub stars is low but it is an early official release.
capabilities.security_note: Connects to https://mcp.datadoghq.com; requires Datadog API credentials. Keep DD_API_KEY and DD_APP_KEY in secrets manager, never in config files.

capabilities.what_it_is: Snowflake Labs MCP server: Cortex AI, SQL execution, object management, semantic views for Snowflake data cloud
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Snowflake-Labs implementation covering Cortex AI and SQL orchestration; strong fit for enterprise data teams already on Snowflake. Note: original Snowflake-Labs/mcp repo is deprecated; current active version is the official Snowflake MCP Server.
capabilities.security_note: Requires Snowflake credentials (account, user, private key or password); ensure least-privilege warehouse roles are used.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Supabase MCP server: manage tables, query data, fetch config, run migrations via Supabase Management API
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Officially released by Supabase (Launch Week 14, April 2025); highly popular open-source BaaS platform with broad developer adoption; remote MCP endpoint at mcp.supabase.com.
capabilities.security_note: Uses OAuth-style login via Supabase dashboard; ensure MCP URL tokens are rotated and scoped to least-privilege projects.

capabilities.what_it_is: Atlas Docs MCP server by CartographAI: provides hosted clean markdown documentation for libraries/frameworks to LLMs
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful documentation-fetch capability but overlaps heavily with mcp-context7 (Upstash) which has 100K+ Docker pulls vs atlas-docs 10K+; context7 is the stronger recommendation for doc lookup.
capabilities.security_note: Read-only documentation fetch; no credentials required. Low risk profile.

capabilities.what_it_is: Browserbase MCP server: cloud browser automation via Stagehand — navigate, extract data, screenshot, control sessions
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 9.4K Docker pulls and active development (Stagehand v3.0 released 2025); enables cloud-based headless browser automation without local Playwright setup. Unique capability for web-scraping and testing agents.
capabilities.security_note: Requires BROWSERBASE_API_KEY, BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID, and Gemini API key. Browser sessions can access arbitrary web content; enforce URL allowlists if possible.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Buildkite MCP server: trigger builds, view logs, manage pipelines, access test suites via 24 tools
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Buildkite server (7.1K pulls) but Buildkite is a niche CI/CD platform; include only for teams already using Buildkite. CircleCI and GitHub Actions have broader adoption.
capabilities.security_note: Requires BUILDKITE_API_TOKEN; container runs as unprivileged user (cgr.dev/chainguard/static base). Token should be scoped to read-only unless build triggering is needed.

capabilities.what_it_is: Camunda 7 BPM process engine MCP server: manage process instances, complete tasks, handle incidents via 12 tools
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Community-maintained (lepoco, unverified activity cadence); Camunda 7 is end-of-life in October 2025, limiting long-term value. Niche workflow automation use case.
capabilities.security_note: Connects to Camunda REST API; ensure API credentials are stored as secrets. Camunda 7 EOL in Oct 2025 means security patches may cease.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official CircleCI MCP server: query pipelines, retrieve build logs, identify flaky tests, get branch status
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official CircleCI-Public release but CircleCI's market share is declining vs GitHub Actions; 7.1K pulls on Docker. Include only for CircleCI-native teams.
capabilities.security_note: Requires CircleCI API token; scope token to read-only for log/status queries unless pipeline triggering is required.

capabilities.what_it_is: Context7 MCP server by Upstash: fetches current versioned library/framework docs for LLMs to prevent hallucinated APIs
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 100K+ Docker pulls, 57.8K GitHub stars on the underlying Context7 repo; solves a critical AI-dev problem (stale training data causing wrong API usage). High-value, low-risk addition to any AI coding setup.
capabilities.security_note: Read-only doc fetches via Upstash-hosted API; no user credentials stored. Low risk. Verify docs are sourced from official library repos.

capabilities.what_it_is: Docker Hub MCP server: search/discover Docker images, manage repositories and tags via natural language using Hub APIs
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Docker org release (Nov 2025, Apache 2.0); useful for container-heavy workflows but overlaps with CLI usage. Moderate pull counts; a nice-to-have rather than essential.
capabilities.security_note: HUB_PAT_TOKEN env var for authenticated write operations; use a scoped PAT with minimum required permissions (read-only if only searching).

capabilities.what_it_is: Reference Git MCP server by modelcontextprotocol: local git repo operations — commit, branch, diff, log, status
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Maintained by the MCP reference implementation org (Anthropic-adjacent); covers fundamental local git operations needed by virtually every developer workflow. High-value foundational tool.
capabilities.security_note: Mounts local directories into Docker container; ensure volume mounts are scoped to intended repos only. No credentials required for local ops.

capabilities.what_it_is: NPM package security scanner MCP server providing AI-powered vulnerability, dependency, and license analysis
capabilities.security_note: Offers built-in input validation against Path Traversal, SSRF, and Command Injection; community-authored but Docker-scanned image
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Niche tool for npm-specific security auditing; low pull count (~355/week on Docker Hub); not broadly applicable enough for a curated starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: Microsoft Playwright MCP server enabling AI agents to automate browsers via structured accessibility snapshots
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by Microsoft; Docker-scanned image; browser automation carries inherent SSRF/data-exfil risk in untrusted environments
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 1M+ Docker pulls, Microsoft-maintained, covers a high-value browser automation use-case that underpins E2E testing and web scraping workflows

capabilities.what_it_is: Official modelcontextprotocol reference server implementing structured sequential reasoning for complex problem decomposition
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by the MCP reference org (Anthropic-affiliated); no external API access, minimal attack surface
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 100K+ Docker pulls, official MCP reference implementation, broadly useful for any agentic workflow requiring structured reasoning; low risk

capabilities.what_it_is: SmartBear official MCP server exposing 156 tools for BugSnag, Swagger/API Hub, Reflect, QMetry, Zephyr, PactFlow, and more
capabilities.security_note: Official SmartBear build; Docker-scanned image; 156 exposed tools is a large surface area requiring careful permission scoping
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Only 6.4K pulls; highly specialized for SmartBear ecosystem users; too niche for a general curated starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: Official SonarSource MCP server enabling AI agents to query SonarQube/SonarCloud for code quality gates, issues, and SCA results
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by SonarSource (official vendor); 500K+ Docker pulls; connects to SonarQube API so token hygiene is critical
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official SonarSource server with 500K+ pulls; integrates code quality feedback into AI coding workflows, high practical value for CI-aware teams

capabilities.what_it_is: Kubeshop Testkube MCP server for AI-driven Kubernetes-native test orchestration, execution, and result analysis
capabilities.security_note: Authored by kubeshop (open-source org); Docker-scanned image; requires Kubernetes cluster access, significant privilege consideration
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Only 4.3K pulls; K8s-specific scope limits applicability outside cloud-native shops; not suitable for a general starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: Official modelcontextprotocol reference server providing time queries, timezone conversion, and date arithmetic tools for LLMs
capabilities.security_note: No external API calls; minimal attack surface; official MCP reference implementation packaged by Docker
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 500K+ Docker pulls, official MCP reference server, fills a fundamental LLM gap (temporal awareness) with zero credential risk

capabilities.what_it_is: Asana official V2 remote MCP server (mcp.asana.com/v2/mcp) for task and project management via OAuth 2.0 and Streamable HTTP
capabilities.security_note: OAuth 2.0 with workspace-scoped authorization; V1 deprecated May 2026; official vendor server with no self-hosting required
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and well-maintained but Asana is one of several project-management tools; inclusion depends on whether the target user base uses Asana specifically

capabilities.what_it_is: GitHub's official MCP server (Go-based, co-developed with Anthropic) for repos, issues, PRs, code search, and Actions
capabilities.security_note: Official GitHub product; token-scoped PAT or GitHub App auth; 30.8K GitHub stars; high value target if token is over-permissioned
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 30.8K GitHub stars, official GitHub+Anthropic co-development, covers the dominant SCM platform used by most developers — essential for any dev-focused starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: GitLab built-in MCP server (no install needed) exposing 15 tools for issues, MRs, pipelines, and CI via GitLab Duo at Premium/Ultimate tier
capabilities.security_note: Embedded in GitLab SaaS and self-managed; no separate token needed beyond existing GitLab session; Premium/Ultimate gating limits free-tier access
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and actively maintained but gated to Premium/Ultimate tier; narrower user base than GitHub MCP; relevant for GitLab-first shops only

capabilities.what_it_is: Linear's official hosted MCP server (mcp.linear.app/sse) providing 21 tools for issues, projects, teams, and comments via OAuth
capabilities.security_note: OAuth-only authentication; hosted and managed by Linear; no self-hosting required; minimal local attack surface
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Linear-hosted server with OAuth, covers a widely-used issue tracker popular with engineering teams; low friction to integrate

capabilities.what_it_is: Sentry's official MCP server for querying errors, issues, traces, and performance data from Sentry.io via OAuth
capabilities.security_note: OAuth-based, Cloudflare Workers-backed remote MCP; maintained by getsentry org; 734 GitHub stars; minimal local attack surface
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Sentry server covering error monitoring — a core observability need for most software teams; OAuth auth is low-friction and secure

capabilities.what_it_is: Shopify's official Dev MCP server providing AI access to Shopify API schemas, docs search, and code generation for Admin/Storefront APIs
capabilities.security_note: Runs locally, no authentication required (read-only doc/schema access); published as @shopify/dev-mcp on npm by Shopify
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and actively maintained, but highly domain-specific (Shopify app developers only); not broadly applicable to a general curated starter set

capabilities.what_it_is: Box MCP server providing 14 tools for listing, reading, downloading, and managing files and folders in Box cloud storage via OAuth 2.0
capabilities.security_note: OAuth 2.0 auth; Docker-scanned image; maintained by individual contributor (dgageot, not Box Inc. directly) — verify official Box endorsement before enterprise use
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Only 6.9K pulls; community-authored (not Box Inc. official); low adoption makes it unsuitable for a curated starter set without further vetting

capabilities.what_it_is: Official MCP reference server for local filesystem access with configurable allowed paths (read/write/search/metadata, 11 tools)
capabilities.security_note: Restricts access to pre-configured paths; Docker MCP Catalog image. Security caveat: exposes local filesystem to LLM — path scoping must be carefully configured to prevent unintended access.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Core Anthropic reference server used by nearly every developer onboarding to MCP; high utility for local file workflows.

capabilities.what_it_is: Microsoft MarkItDown MCP server: converts PDFs, Office docs, images, HTML and other formats to Markdown via a single convert_to_markdown tool
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by Microsoft (github.com/microsoft/markitdown); Docker image has 100k+ pulls. Processes local and remote URIs (http/https/file/data); validate URI inputs to prevent SSRF.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Broadly useful document-ingestion tool from a verified Microsoft source; 100k+ Docker pulls indicate wide adoption.

capabilities.what_it_is: Community-packaged Notion MCP server (Docker catalog); provides Notion workspace CRUD via 22 tools (pages, databases, blocks, comments)
capabilities.security_note: Requires INTERNAL_INTEGRATION_TOKEN; Docker MCP Catalog image. Distinct from makenotion-notion-mcp-server (official-vendor entry) — same upstream code but different distribution channel.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Superseded by makenotion-notion-mcp-server (official-vendor entry); prefer that for the curated set to avoid duplication.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Figma MCP server (remote, hosted at mcp.figma.com): design-to-code, code-to-design, canvas write, and design system integration
capabilities.security_note: Remote server hosted by Figma at https://mcp.figma.com/mcp; requires Figma auth. Write-to-canvas features require a paid Figma plan. Official-vendor tier means Figma controls the endpoint.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Only first-party Figma MCP; critical for design-to-code workflows and uniquely enables AI agents to write directly to Figma canvas.

capabilities.what_it_is: Anthropic-hosted Claude connector for Google Drive: search and access files from Google Drive in Claude conversations
capabilities.security_note: Operated by Anthropic as a remote MCP connector in the Claude Connectors Directory. Requires Google OAuth grant. Anthropic holds the connector endpoint; review access scopes before enabling.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: First-party Anthropic connector with broad end-user demand; enables file search directly in Claude.ai without self-hosting.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Notion MCP server by Notion (makenotion): full Notion API access with 22 tools for pages, databases, blocks, comments, and data sources
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by Notion at github.com/makenotion/notion-mcp-server; v2.0.0 targets Notion API 2025-09-03. Requires INTERNAL_INTEGRATION_TOKEN — scope the integration to only the pages the agent needs.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Notion vendor server actively updated to latest Notion API versions; preferred over the docker-catalog mcp-notion for production use.

capabilities.what_it_is: Anthropic reference knowledge-graph memory server: persistent entity/relation/observation storage backed by a local JSON file (@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory)
capabilities.security_note: Official MCP reference server from the modelcontextprotocol org; 74k+ npm downloads. Stores memory as plaintext JSON — secure the file path and restrict access to trusted agents only.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Core reference server for agent memory; high adoption, maintained by Anthropic's MCP org, essential for stateful agent workflows.

capabilities.what_it_is: Neo4j-backed knowledge graph memory MCP server: persistent entity/relation storage with fulltext search via Neo4j graph DB (9 tools)
capabilities.security_note: Requires NEO4J_URL and credentials; Docker MCP Catalog image maintained by neo4j-contrib. More scalable than JSON-file mcp-memory for large knowledge graphs; Neo4j database must be separately secured.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Powerful upgrade over mcp-memory for production-scale agents, but adds Neo4j infrastructure dependency; better as optional add-on than base set entry.

capabilities.what_it_is: Apify MCP server: exposes Apify Actors marketplace for web scraping, data extraction, and browser automation to AI agents
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by Apify (github.com/apify/apify-mcp-server); Docker MCP Catalog image. Requires APIFY_TOKEN. Grants agent access to thousands of web scrapers — scope allowed Actors carefully and validate outputs.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful for web automation and data extraction use cases but specialized; better suited as an opt-in tool than a universal starter set entry.

capabilities.what_it_is: Brave Search MCP server: privacy-focused web search, news, and local POI search using Brave's LLM Context API (optimized for RAG/grounding)
capabilities.security_note: Requires BRAVE_API_KEY; Docker MCP Catalog image. Official upstream at modelcontextprotocol/servers. Privacy-preserving search with no cross-site tracking — lower data-leakage risk than Google-based alternatives.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: High-demand web search capability with a privacy-respecting backend; one of the most commonly recommended MCP search integrations.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Elastic MCP server for Elasticsearch: list indices, query, search, and analyze data across Elasticsearch via MCP tools
capabilities.security_note: Maintained by Elastic (github.com/elastic/mcp-server-elasticsearch); Docker MCP Catalog image. Requires ES_URL and ES_API_KEY. Only expose read-only API keys to the agent unless write operations are explicitly required.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Highly relevant for Elasticsearch-heavy stacks but too infrastructure-specific for a general curated starter set.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official MCP reference fetch server: retrieves any URL and converts HTML to Markdown for LLM consumption; supports robots.txt and proxy configuration
capabilities.security_note: Official Anthropic MCP reference server (modelcontextprotocol/servers); Docker MCP Catalog image. Security warning from official docs: can access local/internal IP addresses — run in a sandboxed network and restrict internal SSRF vectors.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Fundamental web-fetching primitive used by nearly all agentic workflows; official Anthropic reference implementation with wide adoption.

capabilities.what_it_is: Perplexity AI MCP server: real-time web search and deep research via Perplexity Sonar models (conversational, reasoning, and deep-research tools)
capabilities.security_note: Requires PERPLEXITY_API_KEY; Docker MCP Catalog image. Upstream source is ppl-ai/modelcontextprotocol on GitHub. All queries are sent to Perplexity's API — review data-sharing implications for sensitive prompts.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Strong real-time research capability but overlaps with mcp-brave-search; prefer brave-search in the base set (no per-query cost) and offer perplexity-ask as a premium add-on.

capabilities.what_it_is: HashiCorp official MCP server for Terraform registry browsing, module lookup, provider schema inspection, and policy documentation.
capabilities.security_note: Official HashiCorp repo; read-only registry access, no credentials required for public registry browsing. If Terraform Cloud integration is used, scope TFC_TOKEN to minimum permissions.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official HashiCorp server covering the dominant IaC platform; broadly useful for infrastructure teams and AI-assisted Terraform authoring workflows.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Firecrawl MCP server for JS-rendered web scraping, full-site crawling, and structured data extraction from dynamic pages.
capabilities.security_note: Official vendor server from Mendable/Firecrawl. Requires FIRECRAWL_API_KEY. Crawls arbitrary URLs — validate URL inputs to prevent SSRF against internal networks.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: One of the most-referenced scraping MCPs in 2026; handles JS-rendered pages that mcp-fetch cannot, filling a critical gap for modern web automation agents.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Tavily MCP server providing AI-optimized real-time web search, URL content extraction, and site crawl tools tuned for RAG pipelines.
capabilities.security_note: Official Tavily vendor server. Requires TAVILY_API_KEY. All queries leave the local environment — avoid sending sensitive context in search queries.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Widely adopted as the default search backend for RAG and agent pipelines; AI-optimized result format reduces post-processing needed by the LLM.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Exa MCP server for semantic web search and retrieval infrastructure, optimized for AI agents needing deep research and knowledge retrieval.
capabilities.security_note: Official Exa Labs vendor server. Requires EXA_API_KEY. Semantic search results may include third-party content; validate before acting on retrieved data.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Strong semantic search capability but overlaps with Tavily and Brave Search; better as an opt-in alternative for research-heavy workflows than a universal base inclusion.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Jina AI MCP server for converting any URL to clean Markdown optimized for LLM ingestion via the Jina Reader API.
capabilities.security_note: Official Jina AI vendor server. Requires JINA_API_KEY for rate-limit-free access (free tier available without key). URL fetching carries SSRF risk — restrict to external URLs only.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Lightweight URL-to-markdown conversion; useful but largely superseded by mcp-fetch for basic cases and Firecrawl for JS-heavy pages. Good as an opt-in complement.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Redis Inc. MCP server for key-value operations, pub/sub messaging, stream processing, and caching against Redis instances.
capabilities.security_note: Official Redis Inc. vendor server. Requires REDIS_URL (include AUTH token in URI). Redis instances should not be publicly exposed without authentication; use TLS in transit.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Redis server covering the most widely used in-memory data store; essential for agent memory, caching, and pub/sub workflows.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official MotherDuck MCP server for in-process SQL analytics against local DuckDB files or cloud-connected MotherDuck databases.
capabilities.security_note: Official MotherDuck/DuckDB vendor server. Requires MOTHERDUCK_TOKEN for cloud mode; local DuckDB mode needs no credentials. SQL execution can modify local files — restrict to read-only roles where possible.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Excellent lightweight analytics option for local data workflows, but narrower use case than general-purpose databases. Best as an opt-in for data-heavy or analytics agent setups.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official dbt Labs MCP server for running dbt commands, browsing models and lineage graphs, inspecting test results, and interacting with dbt Cloud.
capabilities.security_note: Official dbt Labs vendor server. May require DBT_CLOUD_SERVICE_TOKEN for dbt Cloud features. dbt run commands can execute SQL against production warehouses — enforce environment segregation.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Essential for modern data stack teams using dbt, but highly specialized. Valuable opt-in for analytics engineers rather than a universal base inclusion.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Google MCP server for BigQuery: running SQL queries, managing datasets, and inspecting table schemas in Google Cloud.
capabilities.security_note: Official Google vendor server. Requires GCP credentials (service account or ADC). BigQuery queries incur cost and can access sensitive enterprise data — enforce least-privilege IAM roles and dataset-level access controls.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Google/BigQuery server but highly GCP-specific; strong for GCP-centric data teams but not broadly applicable enough for a general starter set.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Google/Looker MCP server for executing Looks, browsing Explores, running LookML queries, and accessing BI dashboards.
capabilities.security_note: Official Google/Looker vendor server. Requires LOOKER_BASE_URL, LOOKER_CLIENT_ID, and LOOKER_CLIENT_SECRET. Treat credentials as secrets; Looker access can expose sensitive business intelligence data.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and well-maintained but narrowly scoped to Looker BI users; too niche for a general curated starter set.

capabilities.what_it_is: MCP server that exposes MindsDB as a unified AI-queryable interface across 200+ databases and data platforms via natural language SQL.
capabilities.security_note: MindsDB org maintains the server but it is not independently security-scanned. Connects to potentially many downstream data sources — verify connection credentials are scoped to read-only roles. 39K GitHub stars on parent repo is for the broader MindsDB project, not MCP specifically.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Impressive breadth (200+ integrations) but the MindsDB MCP layer is newer and unverified; trust is Community-Unverified pending security scan. Better as opt-in for data-federation use cases.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Google open-source MCP server providing fast, secure database tooling across 12+ database engines (AlloyDB, BigQuery, Spanner, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and more) via a single binary.
capabilities.security_note: Official Google open-source project. Database credentials are configured server-side in a YAML config file — protect this file as a secret. Supports IAM-based auth for GCP databases; enforce least-privilege per database.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Google toolbox with 16K+ stars covering 12+ DB engines in a single binary; exceptional breadth and strong Google backing make it a compelling universal database MCP choice.

capabilities.what_it_is: Built-in MCP endpoint in Netdata for infrastructure monitoring: discover nodes, explore metrics, run queries, and perform root-cause analysis across the full observability dataset.
capabilities.security_note: Official Netdata project (79K+ stars on parent repo). MCP endpoint ships with every Netdata install — secure the Netdata API with authentication tokens and restrict to trusted networks. Read-only by default.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Compelling for Netdata users (ships for free with every install) but niche compared to Datadog/Grafana which have broader enterprise adoption. Best as opt-in for Netdata-native infrastructure.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official New Relic MCP server for querying APM metrics, infrastructure data, browser monitoring, and executing NRQL against New Relic One.
capabilities.security_note: Official New Relic vendor server. Requires NEW_RELIC_API_KEY and NEW_RELIC_ACCOUNT_ID. Scope API key to minimum required capabilities; avoid using full-admin keys.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and well-maintained but New Relic has smaller market share than Datadog/Grafana; relevant for New Relic-specific teams rather than a universal starter set.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Honeycomb MCP server (hosted endpoint) for structured observability queries, trace analysis, and dataset exploration against Honeycomb.
capabilities.security_note: Official Honeycomb vendor server; self-hosted version deprecated in 2026 in favor of hosted endpoint. Requires HONEYCOMB_API_KEY. Queries may expose sensitive trace data — scope key to read-only datasets.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Niche but valued in distributed-tracing and observability-first orgs. Too specialized for a general starter set; best as opt-in for Honeycomb users.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official PostHog MCP server for product analytics: querying events, funnels, dashboards, cohorts, and feature flag status from PostHog.
capabilities.security_note: Official PostHog vendor server. Requires POSTHOG_API_KEY and POSTHOG_HOST. Scope API key to read-only project access; PostHog data can include personally identifiable user behavior.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Solid official server for PostHog users but product analytics is a specialist need; better as opt-in rather than a universal base inclusion.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Docker MCP server for managing containers, images, volumes, networks, and Docker Desktop from AI agent workflows.
capabilities.security_note: Official Docker vendor server. Requires access to the Docker daemon socket — this grants root-equivalent control over the host. Never expose the Docker socket to untrusted agents; use Docker contexts and minimal-privilege setups.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Docker server covering the most widely used container runtime; essential for DevOps and container-centric development agent workflows.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Slack MCP server (slackapi org) for message search, channel reading, posting messages, and Slack workspace management — replaces the archived Anthropic reference implementation.
capabilities.security_note: Official Slack API org (slackapi/mcp-server-slack). Requires SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_TEAM_ID. Posting capability can exfiltrate data to Slack channels — scope bot to minimum required channels and restrict write permissions where possible.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Slack vendor server superseding the archived reference implementation; Slack is the dominant team communication platform and this is the correct current server to use.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Atlassian remote MCP server (hosted) covering Jira, Confluence, and Compass with 72+ tools via OAuth — distinct from the popular community sooperset server.
capabilities.security_note: Official Atlassian vendor server using OAuth 2.0. Remote-hosted by Atlassian; no self-hosting required. Write operations (create issue, update page) can modify production project data — use human-in-the-loop confirmation for destructive actions.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Atlassian alternative to the community sooperset server already in the seed; useful for teams requiring vendor-official SLA, but the community version has higher adoption and more tools. Add when official vendor trust is required.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Microsoft MCP server covering Outlook email, Calendar events, Teams messages, and OneDrive file access for enterprise M365 workflows.
capabilities.security_note: Official Microsoft vendor server. Requires Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) OAuth credentials. Grants access to email, calendar, and files — scope to minimum required Microsoft Graph permissions and review data residency implications.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Microsoft server for the dominant enterprise productivity suite; high everyday utility for organizations running M365, covering email, calendar, and Teams in one server.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official ClickUp MCP server with ~49 tools for tasks, docs, goals, spaces, and project management across the ClickUp platform.
capabilities.security_note: Official ClickUp vendor server. Requires CLICKUP_API_TOKEN. Write operations can create/modify/delete workspace data — use a token scoped to the minimum required workspaces.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official ClickUp server with growing adoption, but ClickUp has smaller market share than Notion/Linear/Jira; better as opt-in for ClickUp-native teams.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Salesforce MCP server with 60+ tools for records, SOQL queries, workflow automation, Apex execution, and CRM operations — most comprehensive CRM MCP available.
capabilities.security_note: Official Salesforce vendor server. Requires SALESFORCE_USERNAME, SALESFORCE_PASSWORD, SALESFORCE_TOKEN (or OAuth). CRM data is highly sensitive — enforce read-only Connected App permissions unless write access is explicitly needed.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Most comprehensive CRM MCP but narrowly scoped to Salesforce users; too enterprise-specific for a general curated starter set. Essential opt-in for Salesforce-centric orgs.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Pipedream MCP server connecting AI agents to 2,500+ APIs with 8,000+ prebuilt automation tools, enabling broad third-party integrations without individual API setup.
capabilities.security_note: Official PipedreamHQ vendor server. Requires PIPEDREAM_API_KEY. Grants access to a very large surface area of third-party APIs — review which integrations are enabled and apply principle of least privilege per connected app.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 11K+ GitHub stars; unique breadth of 2,500+ API integrations in a single server. Compelling for automation-heavy agent setups where connecting to many services quickly is the goal.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Google Workspace MCP server for Drive, Docs, Sheets, Gmail, and Calendar access — vendor-maintained successor to the archived Anthropic reference implementations.
capabilities.security_note: Official Google vendor server. Requires Google OAuth credentials with appropriate Workspace scopes. Access to Drive and Docs can expose sensitive organizational documents — scope OAuth to minimum required APIs.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Google replacement for the archived Anthropic reference Workspace MCP; covers the most widely used document and productivity suite globally with a single server.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official 1Password MCP server backed by the 1Password CLI for secure credential retrieval and secret management in agent workflows — avoids hardcoded secrets in configs.
capabilities.security_note: Official 1Password vendor server. Requires 1Password CLI (`op`) and OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN. The service account token itself is a high-value secret — store it in the system keychain or environment, never in config files. Scope vault access to minimum required items.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official 1Password server representing best-practice secret management for MCP setups; prevents the antipattern of hardcoded API keys in agent configurations.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Snyk MCP server for vulnerability scanning, license compliance checking, and security issue triage across codebases and container images.
capabilities.security_note: Official Snyk vendor server. Requires SNYK_TOKEN. Scan results are sent to Snyk's cloud — review data-sharing implications for proprietary codebases. Read-only scanning posture; no code modification.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official security scanning server from a leading vendor; integrating vulnerability detection directly into AI coding agent workflows is high-value for any team shipping software.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Auth0/Okta MCP server for identity management: inspecting applications, configuring connections, managing users, and administering Auth0 tenants.
capabilities.security_note: Official Auth0/Okta vendor server. Requires AUTH0_DOMAIN and AUTH0_CLIENT_ID/SECRET (M2M application). IAM operations are highly privileged — restrict Management API permissions to only the actions the agent needs and prefer read-only scopes for exploration.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official and well-maintained but IAM administration is a high-risk, narrow use case; security-sensitive enough to warrant explicit opt-in rather than default inclusion.

capabilities.what_it_is: Community guardrail proxy MCP server that wraps any other MCP server and blocks destructive tool calls before execution, acting as a safety layer for AI coding agents.
capabilities.security_note: Community project at very early stage (20 GitHub stars). A-rated on Glama registry but not independently security-scanned. Ironically, a security proxy itself must be trusted completely — review source code before deploying in any production pipeline.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Interesting guardrail concept but only 20 stars and unverified maintenance cadence; too immature for a curated starter set. Monitor for growth and conduct security scan before adopting.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Qdrant MCP server for vector search, collection management, point upsert, and similarity operations against Qdrant vector databases.
capabilities.security_note: Official Qdrant vendor server. Requires QDRANT_URL and optionally QDRANT_API_KEY. Qdrant collections may contain sensitive embedded data — ensure the Qdrant instance is not publicly accessible without authentication.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official server for one of the leading vector databases; vector search is a foundational capability for RAG agent architectures, and Qdrant is a top choice in the space.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Pinecone MCP server for vector upsert, semantic query, namespace management, and index operations against Pinecone vector databases.
capabilities.security_note: Official Pinecone vendor server. Requires PINECONE_API_KEY. Write operations can overwrite or delete vectors — apply confirmation gates for destructive namespace operations.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Pinecone server for production RAG pipelines, but Qdrant and Weaviate are gaining ground; better as an opt-in for Pinecone-specific stacks than a universal base inclusion.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Weaviate MCP server for vector and hybrid search, schema management, object CRUD, and collection operations against Weaviate instances.
capabilities.security_note: Official Weaviate vendor server. Requires WEAVIATE_URL and WEAVIATE_API_KEY. Write operations can modify collection schemas and objects — use read-only API keys when only search is needed.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Weaviate server with hybrid search capability, but narrower adoption than Qdrant/Pinecone in the MCP ecosystem. Good opt-in for Weaviate-native RAG stacks.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official OpenAI MCP connector allowing agents running on any host to call OpenAI models and APIs as MCP tools — enables multi-provider and model-routing architectures.
capabilities.security_note: Official OpenAI vendor server. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY. All requests leave the local environment and are billed to the OpenAI account — monitor usage and set hard spend limits. Never log or persist raw prompts sent through the connector.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful for multi-provider setups but niche for WERKHub users who are Claude-primary; more relevant as opt-in when OpenAI models are needed for specific tasks.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official HuggingFace MCP server for model hub browsing, dataset access, Space interaction, and inference endpoint queries.
capabilities.security_note: Official HuggingFace vendor server. Requires HF_TOKEN for private model/dataset access. Public hub access is read-only; inference via Serverless Inference API incurs costs and sends data to HuggingFace servers.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official HuggingFace server with strong ML research utility, but specialized for model-evaluation and ML workflows. Better as opt-in for ML-heavy agent setups.

capabilities.what_it_is: High-performance code intelligence MCP server for semantic codebase indexing, search, and context retrieval using LSP and vector embeddings.
capabilities.security_note: Community server (deusdata org); not independently security-scanned. Indexes local codebase content and may store embeddings — ensure index files are gitignored and not shared. Review data handling before use with proprietary code.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Interesting code-intelligence capability with 4K stars, but community-unverified; Serena (serena-coding-mcp) offers a competing approach via language servers. Monitor and security-scan before production adoption.

capabilities.what_it_is: ByteDance browser automation MCP server using Puppeteer for local and remote browser control, part of the UI-TARS-desktop agent infrastructure.
capabilities.security_note: ByteDance-maintained but not independently security-scanned. Browser automation carries SSRF and data-exfiltration risk. Being ByteDance-origin warrants additional review in sensitive enterprise environments per local compliance requirements.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 36K GitHub stars on parent repo is impressive, but microsoft/playwright-mcp (already in seed) covers the same browser automation need with a more trusted vendor provenance. Add only if Puppeteer-specific behavior is required.

capabilities.what_it_is: LLM and computer-vision powered web workflow automation server for handling dynamic, JS-heavy pages via visual understanding rather than DOM selectors.
capabilities.security_note: Community project (not vendor-official). 22K stars on parent repo. Requires SKYVERN_API_KEY for cloud mode. Visual automation can interact with any UI element including sensitive forms — apply strict task scoping and review automation workflows before running.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Novel CV-based approach for hard-to-automate pages, but Community-Unverified and overlaps with Playwright for most use cases. Better as opt-in for workflows where DOM-based automation fails.

capabilities.what_it_is: Community MCP server that automates the user's local Chrome browser via CDP, enabling authenticated and stateful browsing with existing session cookies.
capabilities.security_note: Community project; not independently security-scanned. Attaches to an existing Chrome session which may have logged-in accounts — an agent with this tool can act as the user in any authenticated web app. Treat with high caution; restrict to isolated Chrome profiles.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful for authenticated local browser automation but carries significant risk due to access to live authenticated sessions. Use only in explicit, controlled agent workflows with human oversight.

capabilities.what_it_is: AI-powered task management MCP system featuring PRD parsing, task breakdown, dependency tracking, and multi-provider LLM support for agentic project management.
capabilities.security_note: Community project (individual maintainer); not independently security-scanned. Requires API keys for connected LLM providers. PRD content fed into the system may be sent to external LLM APIs — review data handling for sensitive planning documents.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 27K GitHub stars indicates strong community traction but it is community-maintained by an individual. Useful for AI-native project management but overlaps with native Claude task capabilities; better as explicit opt-in.

capabilities.what_it_is: Fully-featured coding agent MCP using LSP language servers for precise, symbol-aware code navigation, refactoring, and editing without full-file reads.
capabilities.security_note: Community project; not independently security-scanned. Runs language server processes locally and modifies source files — restrict to project directories and review proposed changes before applying. High code-modification capability warrants careful scoping.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 25K GitHub stars and a genuinely novel LSP-based approach, but Community-Unverified. Valuable opt-in for advanced coding agent workflows once security posture is reviewed.

capabilities.what_it_is: Unified MCP middleware server that aggregates and manages multiple MCP server connections through a single GUI-configurable interface, acting as a meta-aggregator.
capabilities.security_note: Community project; not independently security-scanned. Acts as a proxy for all connected MCP servers — a vulnerability here affects every downstream server. Review source code before trusting with sensitive server credentials.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Useful infrastructure for managing complex multi-MCP setups but Community-Unverified with 2.4K stars; the aggregator pattern introduces a single point of failure. Better as opt-in for power users managing many servers.

capabilities.what_it_is: Official Replit MCP server for running code, creating and managing Repls, and using Replit's online IDE programmatically from AI agents.
capabilities.security_note: Official Replit vendor server. Requires REPLIT_TOKEN. Code executed in Repls runs in sandboxed Replit containers, which limits local system risk; however, Replit executes arbitrary code in the cloud — validate agent-generated code before execution.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Official Replit server useful for cloud code execution sandboxing but narrowly scoped to Replit platform users. Good opt-in for education and prototyping agent workflows.

capabilities.what_it_is: Widely-adopted community MCP server (GLips/Figma-Context-MCP, endorsed by Figma) providing design file access, component inspection, and design-to-code context via the Figma REST API.
capabilities.security_note: Community project (individual maintainer); not independently security-scanned despite Figma endorsement. Requires FIGMA_API_KEY. Read-only access to Figma files — still treat the API key as a secret and scope to minimum required files.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: 15K stars and de-facto standard before the official Figma MCP launched; the official figma-com-mcp (already in seed) is now preferred. This community version remains useful for local/self-hosted setups where the official remote server is not suitable.

capabilities.what_it_is: Community MCP server providing AI-ready access to US/UK nonprofit data and open-source software vulnerability intelligence via 10 tools, requiring no API key.
capabilities.security_note: Community project; not independently security-scanned. No API key required means no authentication gate — data quality and freshness depends entirely on maintainer. Treat vulnerability data as advisory; verify against primary sources (NVD, OSV) before acting.
capabilities.deluxe_reason: Novel public-interest data access with no key requirement, but maintenance status is unknown and the use case is narrow. Interesting opt-in for compliance or nonprofit-sector agent workflows.
